fact: The Yellow River's dikes were built higher than the surrounding floodplain, so the river flowed above rooftop level in many areas
source: The Yellow River: A Natural and Unnatural History, Ruth Mostern (2021); also widely documented in historical hydrology sources

fact: The 1887 flood was triggered by a breach in the south dike of the Yellow River near Zhengzhou, Henan Province
source: Wikipedia, citing contemporary reports and Chinese historical records

fact: The floodwaters inundated approximately eleven hundred villages
source: International Red Cross historical records and contemporary missionary accounts; summarized in multiple encyclopedic sources

fact: An estimated 900,000 people died in the 1887 Yellow River flood
source: Widely documented; estimates range from 900,000 to 2,000,000. See: Wikipedia summary of the 1887 Yellow River flood and EM-DAT International Disaster Database

fact: Weeks of heavy rain preceded the dike breach, and Qing dynasty officials failed to allocate adequate repair funds for the deteriorating levees
source: Dodgen, Randall A., Controlling the Dragon: Confucian Engineers and the Yellow River in Late Imperial China (2001), University of Hawai'i Press
